content.problemStatement:
One taught me love 

One taught me patience 

And one taught me pain 

Now, I'm so amazing

You have an undirected graph GGG with NNN nodes and MMM edges.

There are KKK mail carriers such that the ithi^{th}ith carrier is initially on node XiX_iXi‚Äč and can deliver mail to any node having **at most** DiD_iDi‚Äč distance from node XiX_iXi‚Äč.

Note that the distance between two nodes is defined as the total number of **nodes** on the shortest path between them. For instance, the distance between the nodes directly connected by a single edge is 222.

Find whether the mail carriers can deliver mails to all nodes.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM, KKK ‚ÄĒ the number of nodes, the number of edges, and the number of mail carriers.
The next line of each test case contains KKK space-separated integers XiX_iXi‚Äč - the indices of the nodes that contain a mail carrier.
The next line of each test case contains KKK space-separated integers DiD_iDi‚Äč - the distance that the ithi^{th}ith mail carrier can travel.
The next MMM lines will contain two space-separated integers each, uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč, representing an edge between nodes uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if the mail can be delivered to every node, and `NO` otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase. That is, the strings `NO`, `no`, `nO`, and `No` will be treated as equivalent.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.
The sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are 333 nodes, 333 edges, and 222 carriers. The carrier at node 111 can travel at most 111 unit distance and the carrier at node 333 can travel at most 222 unit distance.
Thus, the carrier at node 333 can deliver mails to all nodes.
Test case 222: There are 333 nodes, 333 edges, and 111 carrier. The carrier at node 111 can travel at most 111 unit distance.
Thus, the carrier at node 111 can deliver mail to node 111 only and the nodes 222 and 333 are non-reachable.
